Benchmark.title: STIG Baseline for python Container Image
Benchmark.description:
This STIG baseline template applies DISA Security Technical Implementation Guide controls to the python container image (Debian Bookworm with Python 3.12 and wolfSSL FIPS).
Container Scope: - Application runtime security - Cryptographic enforcement - Audit logging - Access controls - Package management
Host Responsibilities (Out of Scope): - Kernel hardening - Boot process security - Physical security - Host network configuration - Host-level services

Profile.title: Container FIPS Baseline
Profile.description: Baseline profile for FIPS-compliant containerized applications on Debian Bookworm. Includes only controls applicable to container runtime environments.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014
Group.description: The Debian operating system must implement FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.

Rule.title: Debian must use FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.
Rule.description:
Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. The operating system must implement cryptographic modules adhering to the higher standards approved by the federal government.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) - Python 3.12 runtime with ssl module - wolfProvider v1.0.2 - OpenSSL 3.0+ provider routing to wolfSSL FIPS - OpenSSL 3.0.18 provider interface with FIPS property filtering

check.check-content:
Verify FIPS mode is enabled in the container:
1. Check OpenSSL configuration: $ docker exec <container> cat /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf | grep -A5 "libwolfprov"
Expected output: [libwolfprov_sect] activate = 1 module = /usr/local/lib/libwolfprov.so
[algorithm_sect] default_properties = fips=yes
2. Verify Python ssl module reports OpenSSL with wolfProvider: $ docker exec <container> python3 -c "import ssl; print(ssl.OPENSSL_VERSION)"
Expected: OpenSSL 3.0.18 30 Sep 2025
3. Run FIPS validation test: $ docker run --rm python:3.12-bookworm-slim-fips python3 --version
Expected: Container starts successfully with FIPS KATs passing and provider verification logged.
If FIPS provider is not configured or validation fails, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Ensure container is built with FIPS-validated cryptographic modules:
1. Use wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) 2. Build and install wolfProvider v1.0.2 (OpenSSL provider) 3. Configure /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf: - providers = provider_sect - libwolfprov = libwolfprov_sect - activate = 1 - module = /usr/local/lib/libwolfprov.so - default_properties = fips=yes (CRITICAL: enables FIPS property filtering) 4. Verify with /test-fips executable and integrity-check.sh 5. Confirm Python ssl module uses OpenSSL 3.0.18
Rebuild container if FIPS mode is not properly configured.

check.fixtext: Configure FIPS mode for the container

reference.dc:title: POC Validation Report
reference.dc:subject: Test Case 3: OS FIPS Status Check

Group.title: SRG-OS-000396-GPOS-00176
Group.description: The Debian operating system must block non-FIPS approved cryptographic algorithms.

Rule.title: Debian must block MD5 and weak cryptographic hash functions.
Rule.description:
Unapproved mechanisms that are used for authentication to the cryptographic module are not verified and therefore cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity.
Container Implementation: - MD5: Blocked at OpenSSL EVP API level via FIPS property filtering (default_properties=fips=yes) - 3DES, RC4, DES, DSA: Blocked by wolfProvider (0 cipher suites available) - SHA-1 for new TLS: Blocked (0 SHA-1 cipher suites) - SHA-1 for verification: Available (legacy cert verification - FIPS 140-3 compliant) - Enforcement layer: OpenSSL 3.0.18 provider + openssl.cnf configuration

check.check-content:
Verify non-FIPS algorithms are blocked:
1. Test MD5 blocking at OpenSSL level: $ docker exec <container> bash -c "echo -n 'test' | openssl dgst -md5"
Expected: Error setting digest (unsupported)
2. Check available cipher suites (should be 14 FIPS-approved only): $ docker exec <container> python3 -c "import ssl; print(len(ssl.create_default_context().get_ciphers()))"
Expected: 14
3. Run FIPS verification test: $ docker exec <container> python3 /diagnostics/test-fips-verification.py
Expected: Test 6 (Non-FIPS Algorithm Rejection) - PASS: MD5 BLOCKED at OpenSSL level
If MD5 or weak algorithms are available, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure OpenSSL FIPS property filtering:
1. Edit /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf 2. Add algorithm section with FIPS property filtering: [algorithm_sect] default_properties = fips=yes
3. Ensure wolfProvider only marks FIPS-approved algorithms with fips=yes property 4. Verify MD5 blocking: echo -n 'test' | openssl dgst -md5 5. Verify only 14 FIPS cipher suites available
Rebuild container with proper openssl.cnf configuration.

Rule.fixtext: Configure algorithm blocking for the container

reference.dc:title: POC Validation Report
reference.dc:subject: Test Case 1: Algorithm Enforcement via wolfProvider

Group.title: SRG-OS-000342-GPOS-00133
Group.description: The Debian operating system must generate audit records for privileged activities.

Rule.title: Debian must log security-relevant events.
Rule.description:
Audit logging ensures accountability and enables detection of security violations.
Container Implementation: - Entrypoint validation: docker-entrypoint.sh - Library integrity checks: SHA-256 verification via integrity-check.sh - FIPS KAT execution: /test-fips on every startup - Python ssl module verification: fips-init-check.py - Container logs: docker logs (stdout/stderr)

check.check-content:
Verify audit logging is configured:
1. Check entrypoint validation script exists: $ docker exec <container> cat /docker-entrypoint.sh | grep "integrity-check"
Expected: integrity-check.sh execution present
2. View container startup logs: $ docker logs <container-id>
Expected events: - Library checksum verification - FIPS KAT execution - FIPS container verification - Python ssl module validation
3. Verify integrity check script: $ docker exec <container> cat /scripts/integrity-check.sh
Expected: SHA-256 verification of libwolfssl.so and libwolfprov.so
If logging is not configured or events are not logged, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure audit logging:
1. Ensure docker-entrypoint.sh executes validation checks 2. Ensure integrity-check.sh validates library checksums 3. Ensure /test-fips runs FIPS KATs on startup 4. Verify all validation output goes to stdout/stderr 5. Container should exit on validation failure (fail-fast)
Rebuild container with proper entrypoint and validation scripts.

Rule.fixtext: Configure audit logging for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
Group.description: The Debian operating system must verify package integrity.

Rule.title: Debian must verify package and library integrity.
Rule.description:
Package integrity verification ensures that software has not been tampered with.
Container Implementation: - Library checksums: SHA-256 verification via integrity-check.sh - wolfSSL FIPS: In-core integrity hash validated via fips-hash.sh - SBOM generated: SPDX 2.3 format - Image signed: Cosign signature - VEX statement: Vulnerability tracking

check.check-content:
Verify package integrity:
1. Check library integrity script: $ docker exec <container> /scripts/integrity-check.sh
Expected: All integrity checks passed
2. Verify FIPS KAT executable: $ docker exec <container> /test-fips
Expected: FIPS KAT passed successfully
3. Check for SBOM: $ ls compliance/SBOM-python-3.12-bookworm-slim-fips.spdx.json
Expected: File exists
4. Verify image signature: $ cosign verify python:3.12-bookworm-slim-fips
Expected: Signature valid
If integrity cannot be verified, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure package integrity verification:
1. Generate SHA-256 checksums during build 2. Store checksums in /opt/wolfssl-fips/checksums/libraries.sha256 3. Create integrity-check.sh script 4. Execute fips-hash.sh during wolfSSL build 5. Generate SPDX SBOM 6. Sign image with Cosign
Rebuild container with integrity verification enabled.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00228
Group.description: The Debian operating system must run processes as a non-root user.

Rule.title: Debian must enforce least privilege principle.
Rule.description:
Running processes as non-root limits the impact of security vulnerabilities.
Container Implementation: - Container user: appuser (UID 1001) - Group: appuser (GID 1001) - Not running as root

check.check-content:
Verify container runs as non-root:
1. Check USER directive in image: $ docker inspect python:3.12-bookworm-slim-fips | grep User
Expected: "User": "appuser" or "User": "1001"
2. Verify at runtime: $ docker run --rm python:3.12-bookworm-slim-fips id
Expected: uid=1001(appuser) gid=1001(appuser)
If container runs as root (UID 0), this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure non-root user:
1. Create user in Dockerfile: RUN useradd -m -u 1001 appuser 2. Set ownership: RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app 3. Switch user: USER appuser 4. Verify: docker run --rm image id
Rebuild container with non-root user.

Rule.fixtext: Configure non-root user for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00229
Group.description: The Debian operating system must restrict file permissions.

Rule.title: Debian must restrict file permissions to prevent unauthorized access.
Rule.description:
Proper file permissions prevent unauthorized modification of system files.
Container Implementation: - Native libraries: 0644 (libwolfssl.so*, libwolfprov.so*) - Configuration: 0644 (openssl.cnf) - Scripts: 0755 (docker-entrypoint.sh, integrity-check.sh) - No world-writable files

check.check-content:
Verify file permissions:
1. Check for world-writable files: $ docker run --rm python:3.12-bookworm-slim-fips find / -type f -perm -002 2>/dev/null
Expected: No output (no world-writable files)
2. Check sensitive file permissions: $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/lib/libwolfssl.so* $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/lib/libwolfprov.so* $ docker exec <container> ls -la /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf $ docker exec <container> ls -la /scripts/integrity-check.sh
Expected: - Libraries: -rw-r--r-- (0644) - Configuration: -rw-r--r-- (0644) - Scripts: -rwxr-xr-x (0755)
If world-writable files exist or permissions are incorrect, this is a finding.

Rule.fixtext: Configure file permissions for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00230
Group.description: Kernel module loading restrictions (NOT APPLICABLE - Host Responsibility)

Rule.title: Kernel module loading (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Containers share the host kernel and cannot load kernel modules independently. This control is the responsibility of the container host.
Justification: - Containers use the host kernel - No independent kernel module loading capability - Host-level security control
Mitigation: Deploy container on STIG-compliant host with proper kernel hardening.
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. Verify host compliance instead.

fix.fixtext: Configure kernel module restrictions on container host.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00231
Group.description: Boot loader configuration (NOT APPLICABLE - No Boot Process)

Rule.title: Boot loader password (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Containers do not have boot loaders. They are started by container runtime.
Justification: - Containers don't boot; they are started by runtime (Docker, containerd) - No GRUB or boot loader present - Process execution, not boot sequence
Mitigation: N/A - containers use runtime start, not boot.
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. No boot process exists.

fix.fixtext: N/A - containers do not have boot loaders.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00232
Group.description: Systemd service hardening (NOT APPLICABLE - Minimal Container)

Rule.title: Systemd hardening (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Minimal containers use entrypoint scripts, not systemd.
Justification: - Minimal container uses entrypoint (/docker-entrypoint.sh) - No systemd or init system - Single-purpose application container
Mitigation: Process supervision handled by container runtime (Docker, Kubernetes).
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. No systemd present.

fix.fixtext: Use container runtime supervision instead of systemd.
